body.title: [sprint] Testing harness — verify /critic and scripts/critic.py work and find real issues
body.intention: I intend that /critic and scripts/critic.py produce useful, actionable critique before wiring them into the build system.
body.description:
Build a test harness to verify both /critic skill and scripts/critic.py work correctly and produce useful output. Test cases: (1) Known-good code (no issues expected, should say so); (2) Code with intentional bugs (missing error handling, hardcoded paths, silent exceptions); (3) Ticket descriptions with incomplete scope boundaries (should flag); (4) Decisions with questionable assumptions (should surface). Run both /critic and scripts/critic.py on each, compare outputs (should be identical). Spot-check: do the critiques actually find the bugs/gaps we intentionally planted? Are suggestions actionable? Is anything nonsensical/boilerplate? Iterate on prompt until critiques are useful.

**Affected files:** tests/test_critic.py (new), tests/fixtures/critic_test_cases/ (new directory with known-good and buggy code samples)
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Testing only; implementation refinement is separate
**Completion criteria:** (1) Test cases defined and in fixtures; (2) both /critic and scripts/critic.py pass all test cases; (3) outputs match between skill and script; (4) critiques find intentional bugs/gaps (≥80% hit rate); (5) no boilerplate/nonsensical output; (6) test harness is easy to extend with new cases
**Test plan:** Run test suite; inspect outputs manually for quality; iterate on prompt in skill/script if needed
